economic_notes: Band owns multiple commercial enterprises including McGraw Seafood, MicMac Industries, River of Fire Market, Elsipogtog Pharmacy, 711394 NB Inc., McGraw Peat Moss, McGraw Aquaculture, Mlsegnatigeoei Inc., Fire & Ice Retail Inc., and Big Cove Wellness Committee Inc. Commercial fisheries is a major revenue source at $15,517,206.
notes: Elsipogtog First Nation reported a deficit of $2,298,569 for fiscal year 2024 compared to a surplus of $8,993,524 in 2023. Net debt increased significantly from $43,538,022 to $63,716,508. Long-term debt increased from $46,200,316 to $58,529,766. The nation acquired $22,944,242 in tangible capital assets during the year. Several government business enterprises reported losses, most notably McGraw Seafood (2008) Inc. at -$1,373,936. Big Cove Wellness Committee Inc. is fully consolidated while other commercial enterprises use modified equity method. Contributions receivable decreased significantly from $22,639,810 to $11,066,922. Remuneration schedules for Chief, Council and senior officials appear in the document at pages 105-106 but figures were not visible in provided pages.
